headline: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 dominates this cycle as educators race to benchmark and build with the new Mythos class model.
body: Multiple recommended channels converge on Claude Fable 5, a Mythos class model topping SWE Bench Pro and Frontier Code while demonstrating memory, vision, and fullstack capability [7]. Cole Medin runs a live test weighing its added capability against a 2x price premium over Claude Opus 4.8 [0], while Cyril Imhof frames its use in regulated industries through a "Know Your Agent" context engineering methodology [8]. Outside the inner circle, AI Search benchmarks it against GPT 5.5 across 3D reconstruction and medical imaging [11], and Jordan Urbs one-shots a video editing app from screenshots, noting faster token consumption and a June 22 removal date from Claude plans [12]. Separately, NVIDIA's Nemotron 3 ships in Nano, Super, and Ultra sizes with a hybrid Mamba-Transformer and Latent Mixture of Experts design [3], Google's open source Agents CLI adds sandboxed execution and audit trails for shipping agents [4], and IBM Technology covers AI vulnerability detection alongside prompt injection, AI worms, and nonhuman identity breaches [2][6].
